name: Shell-Owned Quit Policy
designPattern: Global Request-Quit Contract / Shell-Level Safe Exit
requirements: Tests ARE the Spec roadmap principle

userStory.asA: user of an interactive framed Bijou app
userStory.iWant: quit keys to mean the same thing everywhere
userStory.soThat: I can leave safely without guessing whether a focused pane, overlay, or shell surface will eat my intent

acceptanceCriteria: Interactive shell surfaces treat `Esc`, `q`, and `Ctrl+C` as request-quit keys routed through one shared policy, with a narrow exception for printable text-entry surfaces that must keep character keys like `q` available for input
Interactive shell surfaces default to a quit-confirm step instead of quitting immediately
Pipe/non-interactive shell contexts quit immediately instead of opening a confirmation overlay
The quit-confirm surface uses stable affordances: `y` or `Enter` to quit, `n`, `q`, or `Esc` to stay
Requesting quit while help, settings, search, or the command palette are open closes those surfaces behind the confirm modal instead of leaving layered shell state active
Search, settings, and help remain closable through their own explicit toggle bindings rather than depending on `Esc` as their only exit path
Search/palette text entry keeps printable query characters available, while `Esc` and `Ctrl+C` still request quit from that surface
DOGFOOD landing uses the same request-quit policy as the framed docs workspace instead of bypassing it with direct `quit()` calls
backwardCompatibility: This is a behavioral tightening for interactive shell apps, not a public data-model break. Existing framed apps keep the same page APIs, but shell key semantics become more consistent and less ad hoc.

inScope: Quit-confirm overlay copy and stable key semantics
Search/palette toggle behavior needed to keep shell surfaces dismissible after Esc becomes request-quit
outScope: Unsaved-work detection or app-specific dirty-state warnings
Persistent preference storage for quit behavior
Non-shell pages inventing their own quit overlays
A generalized confirmation framework beyond the standard quit flow in the same slice

testPlan.goldenPath: Write failing app-frame tests proving that Esc opens quit confirm even while settings or palette are open, that q and Ctrl+C also request quit, and that the shell's explicit surface toggles still close search/settings/help. Then add DOGFOOD preview regressions proving landing Esc opens quit confirm, n dismisses it, y quits, and pipe mode still exits immediately.
edgeCases: Help already open when quit is requested
Settings open when Esc is pressed
Search/command palette open when q or Ctrl+C is pressed
Pipe mode where no interactive confirm should render
Repeated quit requests while the confirm modal is already open
testPlan.fuzzStress: Not a priority in v0. The main risk is behavioral inconsistency and layer precedence, not high-throughput input volume.
failureCases: Esc closes one shell surface but opens quit confirm from another, leaving users unable to predict the outcome
The shell opens quit confirm but leaves search/settings/help active underneath it
Pipe mode accidentally opens a quit modal that can never be answered
DOGFOOD landing still quits immediately while the framed docs route confirms
Search or settings become impossible to dismiss after quit semantics change

estimatedComplexity.linesOfCode: ~150-280 lines across shell helpers, app-frame routing, landing-route adoption, and regressions
estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(1) per key event; the work is in routing precedence and coherent shell state transitions, not algorithmic growth
